rounds.revealFact: Cleopatra VII was the last active ruler of the Ptolemaic Kingdom of Egypt, reigning until 30 BCE.

clues: Fought over three days in Pennsylvania in July 1863.
Often called the turning point of the American Civil War.

events.label: The Ottoman Turks capture Constantinople, ending the Byzantine Empire
Martin Luther posts his 95 Theses, sparking the Reformation
The German states unify into a single empire
The United Nations is founded in San Francisco

image.alt: A large airship engulfed in flames as it descends toward a landing field

rounds.prompt: The RMS Titanic sinks in the North Atlantic after striking an iceberg.
rounds.revealFact: More than 1,500 people died when the supposedly unsinkable liner went down on her maiden voyage.

image.alt: Extreme close-up detail of white marble inlay and carved stonework

rounds.revealFact: Built by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan in memory of his wife Mumtaz Mahal, it was completed around 1653.
